steps.title: Identify the structure
steps.content: The blank joins two independent clauses: 'This is a hypothetical designation' and 'Proto-Indo-European is only postulated, its characteristics being inferred from the linguistic properties of its 448 daughters,' with 'however' marking the contrast. Two valid placements exist for the conjunctive adverb: ending IC1 as a trailing parenthetical (', however;') or starting IC2 ('; however,'). Test which placement the sentence prefers.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'designation however': No punctuation between the two independent clauses — a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice B — 'designation. However,': Splits one continuous contrast into two sentences, misrepresenting the passage's unified structure. ✗
  • Choice C — 'designation; however,': Grammatically valid, but placing 'however' at the start of IC2 frames the hypothetical nature of Proto-Indo-European as a fresh surprising claim rather than qualifying the designation just mentioned. Less idiomatic for this sentence. ✗
  • Choice D — 'designation, however;': The comma binds 'however' to IC1 as a trailing parenthetical, softening 'This is a hypothetical designation' — the word 'however' qualifies that claim directly. The semicolon then joins IC2 explaining why the designation is hypothetical. Parse: [IC1 + trailing 'however']; [IC2]. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
